title: FIFA World Cup Ticket Sales Plagued by Technical Issues and Pricing Concerns

scenes.subtitle_text: FIFA faced technical difficulties during the latest round of World Cup ticket sales, according to The Globe and Mail
scenes.image_search_query: soccer fans entering stadium real life photography

scenes.subtitle_text: The issues arose as fans tried to access the "last-minute sales phase" after the final teams qualified for the expanded 48-team tournament
scenes.image_search_query: crowd of excited soccer fans watching game real life photography

scenes.subtitle_text: Many users were misdirected to a queue intended for supporters of the six nations that qualified on Tuesday, causing significant delays
scenes.image_search_query: frustrated soccer fans waiting in line high quality photo

scenes.subtitle_text: FIFA stated that not all remaining tickets were released and additional tickets will become available on a rolling basis throughout the tournament
scenes.image_search_query: empty seats in soccer stadium real life photography

scenes.subtitle_text: The organization is employing dynamic pricing, which has drawn criticism for potentially making the 2026 World Cup financially inaccessible
scenes.image_search_query: expensive tickets concert show press photo

scenes.subtitle_text: Members of the U.S Congress have voiced concerns that the dynamic pricing starkly contrasts with FIFA's mission to promote accessible soccer globally
scenes.image_search_query: US congress members discussing important issues press photo

scenes.subtitle_text: FIFA also benefits from a 15% commission on both buyer and seller in its own resale market, sparking further debate
scenes.image_search_query: people buying and selling tickets marketplace real life photography
